defenses: Meter was broken or malfunctioning at the time of parking
Payment receipt or ParkMobile/Passport app confirmation shows time had not expired
Meter displayed an error message and could not accept payment
Officer issued the citation before the meter actually expired

P102.description: No parking/standing — prohibited at all times
defenses: Sign was missing, obscured, or illegible at the time of the violation
Vehicle was actively loading or unloading passengers
Emergency circumstances required the stop
No Parking sign placement did not conform to DDOT specifications

P103.description: Rush hour — no parking during designated hours
defenses: Citation was issued outside the posted rush hour restriction hours
Sign was missing, damaged, or illegible at the location
Posted hours were ambiguous or conflicting signs created confusion
Vehicle was gone before the restriction period began per timestamp evidence

P104.description: Residential permit parking — no permit displayed
defenses: Valid residential permit was displayed in the windshield but may have been overlooked
Vehicle is registered at that address and permit had been applied for
Permit was recently issued and sticker had not yet been received
Vehicle is exempt under the DC residential parking rules (e.g., visitor pass was displayed)

P105.description: Street cleaning — no parking during sweeping hours
defenses: Street cleaning was officially suspended on that date (holiday or other suspension)
Sign was missing, obscured, or illegible at the time of the violation
Vehicle was moved before the street sweeper passed
Conflicting or improperly placed signs created genuine ambiguity about the restriction
Vehicle was parked more than 10 feet from the hydrant (measurement dispute)
Hydrant was a private or out-of-service hydrant not subject to the standard restriction
Hydrant location was not clearly visible or marked
Vehicle was attended and would have moved immediately upon request
Vehicle was actively loading or unloading commercial goods at the time
No legal parking space was available within a reasonable distance
Vehicle was attended and positioned to move immediately
Location does not constitute double parking under DC traffic regulations
Registration was renewed prior to or on the date of the violation
Renewal was pending due to a DC DMV processing delay
Documentation shows renewal was submitted before the expiration date
Vehicle is registered in another state and was compliant under that jurisdiction's rules
